methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Lab 91 is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Anand Chamarthy that has raised $10M+. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 12 private peers and 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though the private peer set at this stage and valuation tier is limited.

Of the three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. Using $10M in total raised as a proxy for seed-stage value, a 2.5x multiplier to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $25M. Peer-median analysis was not executable: while 12 same-sector peers exist (including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched), filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies produced insufficient datapoints for reliable triangulation. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied because target revenue is not on file, a typical constraint for private companies; the Semiconductors public median trades at 23.4x P/S across the three tickers, but lacks applicability without revenue disclosure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, centered at $25M, derived from a single method. This narrow methodological foundation reflects acute data constraints in early-stage deep-tech valuations. Key uncertainties include the target's actual revenue trajectory, the timing and terms of subsequent funding rounds, and whether the 2.5x stage multiplier appropriately reflects semiconductor-specific development risk versus broader venture norms.
